architecture_fit.note: Single hand-set positional QK head (no MLP/value), minimal delta from base_model, but k is recovered via cross-batch marker aggregation a real per-sequence head cannot do, inflating attn_at_k to 1.0.

baseline_comparison.note: Content-matching strawman (leaks onto spurious 99s) and uniform 1/L baseline both shown; the strawman failure where the method succeeds is clearly contrasted in the viz.

faithfulness.note: Honestly flagged as hand-built with no model to ablate, and the strawman contrast serves as a route-failure check, but no explicit causal/ablation check is proposed as the rubric asks.

operating_range.note: Perfect across the full required k sweep {0..28} including boundaries, but L/batch fixed and no probing of where the batch-statistics detection would break.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Genuinely hand-set Q/K weights (one-hot query vs identity keys) with no training; the k_hat recovery is a data-dependent computation rather than a static weight, slightly diminishing the elegance.

visual_judgement.note: Clean single-axis mean-attention-vs-position plot for all three methods with a dashed true-k line and a k dropdown; the spike-vs-leakage-vs-flat contrast is immediately legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README 'Why this visualisation' ties the chart to the claim well — smallest flip-the-claim artefact, correct axes, baseline and k-sweep justification.

notes: A clean, minimal, honestly-documented hand-built positional head with strong baselines and a legible visualisation that nails the full k-sweep (attn_at_k=1.0, sharpness≈1.0, zero position bias). The central caveat is that k is detected by aggregating the marker mask across the whole batch — a shortcut a real per-sequence head cannot use — so the perfect metrics partly reflect the eval's batched structure rather than pure content-independent position addressing. Mean of scored items ≈3.6, landing it as borderline despite the polished presentation.
